title: Sweet Spicy Brazilian Chicken Casserole
description: A jammy and gooey festive Brazilian dish featuring enchilada sauce and bhuna, perfect for a Buddhist temple gathering, this Sweet Spicy Brazilian Chicken Casserole is a unique blend of flavors and textures.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat and cook the diced onion and bell pepper until tender.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned and cooked through.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked chicken, enchilada sauce, bhuna sauce, and chopped cilantro.
In a greased 9x13 inch baking dish, arrange half of the cooked rice in the bottom of the dish.
Pour the chicken and sauce mixture over the rice.
Sprinkle half of the shredded cheese over the top of the chicken mixture.
Repeat the layers, starting with the remaining rice, then the remaining chicken mixture, and finally the remaining cheese.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
